name: Power an AI agent task with Slack for your team for Support Bots

options.systemMessage: You answer conversation questions using only the retrieved knowledge-base context. If the context does not contain the answer, say so and set needsHuman=true.

parameters.toolDescription: Semantic search over the support bots knowledge base. Always call this before answering factual conversation questions.

parameters.text: =✅ Power an AI agent task with Slack for your team for Support Bots — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf07734-conversation`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Normalize Conversation Payload

### Normalize Conversation Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming conversation records to a stable schema

Next: Answer from Knowledge Base

### Answer from Knowledge Base

The AI brain: reads the input, decides which attached tools to call (and in what order), then composes the answer.

Ports: ①→ Grounded Answer Found? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Knowledge Base Retriever

Exposes the vector index to the agent as a semantic-search tool.

Feeds **Answer from Knowledge Base** as `tool`

🔑 Postgres connection

### Query Embeddings

Embedding model — MUST be the same model for indexing and querying, or retrieval quality collapses.

Feeds **Knowledge Base Retriever** as `embedding`

🔑 OpenAI API key

### Rerank Retrieved Chunks

Re-scores retrieved chunks so only the most relevant reach the model.

Feeds **Knowledge Base Retriever** as `reranker`

🔑 Cohere API key

### Grounded Answer Found?

Two-way gate on `={{ !($json.output || '').includes('needsHuman') }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Return Answer (HTTP 200) · ②→ Log Knowledge Gap

### Return Answer (HTTP 200)

Sends the HTTP 200 response back to the caller — keep this fast; heavy work happens after/async.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Log Knowledge Gap

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Flag Gap to KB Owners · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Flag Gap to KB Owners

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Flag Gap to KB Owners

parameters.content:
### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Serialize Run Output (CSV)

### Serialize Run Output (CSV)

Serializes items to a CSV binary file.

Next: Archive Row Snapshot

### Archive Row Snapshot

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Fingerprint for Idempotency · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per conversation (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

notion — `getAll` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

### Offload to Post-Process Sub-workflow

Delegates to a reusable sub-workflow (single-responsibility building block) and waits for its result.

Ports: ①→ Record Sub-workflow Result · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Record Sub-workflow Result

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Sort Conversations Newest First

### Sort Conversations Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Guard Non-Empty message

### Guard Non-Empty message

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Align Field Names

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Notion)

### Append Audit Row (Notion)

notion — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Notion integration token

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Power an AI agent task with Slack for your team for Support Bots

**Pattern:** AI Agent · **Level:** Architect · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Power an AI agent task with Slack for your team for Support Bots. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual support bots is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Normalize Conversation Payload → Answer from Knowledge Base → Grounded Answer Found? → Return Answer (HTTP 200) → Stamp Completion Time → Persist Run Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ai agent without manual effort
- Anyone automating support bots in customer support
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Standardizes the process every time
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Cohere API key; Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; Notion integration token; OpenAI API key.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your support bots process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
